name: Chinese Remainder Theorem
description: Chinese Remainder Theorem: If m₁,…,mₖ are pairwise coprime, the system x ≡ a₁ (mod m₁), …, x ≡ aₖ (mod mₖ) has a unique solution modulo M = m₁⋯mₖ.
mermaid:
graph TD
    DefCong["Def: Congruence\na ≡ b mod m"]
    DefCoprime["Def: Pairwise coprime\ngcd(mᵢ,mⱼ)=1 for i≠j"]
    DefSys["Def: System of congruences\nx ≡ aᵢ mod mᵢ"]
    ThmCRT["Thm: CRT\nUnique solution mod M"]
    LemExist["Lem: Existence\nConstruction via Mᵢ, yᵢ"]
    LemUniq["Lem: Uniqueness\nTwo solutions ≡ mod M"]
    AlgEE["Alg: Extended Euclidean\nCompute yᵢ: Mᵢyᵢ ≡ 1 mod mᵢ"]
    AlgEE --> LemExist
    DefCong --> DefSys
    DefCoprime --> ThmCRT
    DefSys --> ThmCRT
    LemExist --> ThmCRT
    LemUniq --> ThmCRT
    classDef definition fill:#b197fc,color:#fff,stroke:#9775fa
    classDef theorem fill:#51cf66,color:#fff,stroke:#40c057
    classDef lemma fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff,stroke:#4dabf7
    class DefCong,DefCoprime,DefSys definition
    class ThmCRT theorem
    class LemExist,LemUniq lemma
